title: Phidias PropFirm Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: Phidias PropFirm Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: Phidias PropFirm Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Phidias PropFirm (3.9★, $55/mo). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Phidias PropFirm — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
Phidias PropFirm is a Gibraltar-based futures-focused proprietary trading firm launched in 2023 that lets traders fund their own accounts through one-time evaluation fees rather than monthly subscriptions. With a 3.9/5 rating and starting prices at $55/month for a $25K account, it's designed for futures day traders and scalpers who want transparent pricing, EOD-based drawdown rules, and high profit splits up to 90%. The firm operates across futures, forex, crypto, and commodities markets, making it a practical choice for traders managing multiple funded accounts without recurring overhead costs.

What is Phidias PropFirm?

Phidias PropFirm is a proprietary trading firm that provides funded trading accounts exclusively for futures trading. Unlike traditional prop firms with monthly fees or recurring charges, Phidias charges a one-time evaluation fee that gets credited toward your account on successful completion. The firm was founded by French traders in 2023 and operates from Gibraltar, bringing a European perspective to the prop trading space.

The firm is best suited for traders who specialize in futures day trading, scalping strategies, and those who want to run multiple funded accounts without drowning in subscription costs. Phidias distinguishes itself through three core mechanics: one-time fees with no monthly charges, end-of-day (EOD) drawdown calculations rather than intraday maximums, and profit splits reaching 90% on live accounts with daily withdrawal options through their proprietary Phidias Wallet.

What attracts traders to Phidias is the cost structure and payout speed. You pay once, pass the evaluation, and then trade your profit split without worrying about monthly data feed subscriptions, platform fees, or activation charges. Payouts arrive in 1–4 hours, which matters if you're day trading and need quick access to your earnings.

How to Create Your Phidias PropFirm Account

Setting up a Phidias PropFirm account is straightforward and takes roughly 10–15 minutes to complete the initial registration:

  • Visit the Phidias website and click the sign-up button. You'll land on a registration page asking for your email address, password, and basic details like your full name and country of residence.
  • Verify your email by clicking the confirmation link sent to your inbox. This step activates your account and lets you log into the dashboard.
  • Complete KYC verification. Phidias requires identity verification before you can fund an account. You'll upload a government-issued ID (passport, driver's license) and proof of address (utility bill, bank statement). Processing typically takes 1–2 business days.
  • Choose your account tier. Decide whether you want the Static 25K ($55), Fundamental 50K ($116), Fundamental 100K ($145), or Fundamental 150K ($173) account. Each tier has different evaluation requirements and profit split structures.
  • Pay the one-time evaluation fee. This fee covers the cost of running your account and is credited to your balance once you pass the evaluation phase. Payment methods include credit card and bank transfer depending on your region.
  • Complete the evaluation. After payment, you'll have access to a demo or evaluation period where you must meet profit targets and follow the trading rules (position limits, drawdown thresholds, news trading on permitted markets). Once approved, your account goes live.

The entire process from sign-up to funded trading account typically takes 3–5 business days, assuming your verification documents are clear and you pass the evaluation phase on your first attempt.

Setting Up Phidias PropFirm for the First Time

Once your account is approved and funded, the setup process involves three main steps: connecting your broker platform, configuring your trading rules, and linking your data feeds.

Dashboard navigation: Log into your Phidias dashboard and you'll see your account balance, current drawdown status, profit/loss for the day, and your payout history. The main tabs include Account (your details and funded balance), Trading Rules (position limits, leverage, restricted symbols), Performance (daily P&L, drawdown tracking), and Wallet (your Phidias Wallet for payouts).

Broker integration setup: Phidias supports Rithmic-compatible platforms. If you're using a platform like ThinkorSwim, you'll need to configure your broker credentials within the Phidias settings. This lets the firm monitor your positions, enforce drawdown rules, and calculate your EOD profit/loss automatically. The integration process requires your broker login and takes about 5 minutes to authorize.

Configure your trading rules: Phidias sets default position limits and leverage for each account tier, but you can customize trading rules within approved bounds. Set your stop-loss thresholds, leverage multiplier, and which markets you'll trade (futures, forex, crypto). The EOD drawdown rule means intraday losses that recover before market close won't trigger a breach—this is a key setting to understand and confirm in your dashboard.

Enable Phidias Wallet: Connect your Phidias Wallet to receive payouts. You'll provide bank details (IBAN or account number depending on your region) and enable two-factor authentication on your wallet for security. Test with a small withdrawal first to ensure your banking details are correct.

Set up alerts: Enable email or push notifications for important events: when you hit 50% of your max drawdown, when a trade violates position limits, and when payouts are processed. These alerts help you stay aware of your account status without checking the dashboard constantly.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. One-Time Evaluation Fee Model: Unlike subscription-based prop firms charging $50–$200/month, Phidias charges a single upfront fee ($55 for a 25K account) that's credited to your trading balance. No monthly data feed costs, activation fees, or recurring charges. This structure saves disciplined traders thousands per year, especially if you run multiple funded accounts.

2. EOD Drawdown Rules: Most prop firms track intraday maximum drawdown—if your account dips 10%, you're out. Phidias uses EOD (end-of-day) drawdown, meaning only the closing drawdown matters. You can be down 15% intraday and recover before close without penalty. This feature is critical for scalpers and day traders who expect intraday volatility but close flat or profitable.

3. Performance Analytics: The Performance Analytics tab shows daily P&L broken down by trading session, win/loss ratio, profit factor, and drawdown history. You can export this data to analyze your edge over time and identify which trading hours, markets, or strategies are most profitable. This granularity helps you optimize which futures contracts or times of day you should focus on.

4. Profit Split Up to 90%: Once live, Phidias offers profit splits ranging from 70–90% depending on your account tier and trading performance. On a $50K account generating $5,000/month profit, you keep $3,500–$4,500 depending on your split. Payouts are daily with no caps, unlike some competitors with monthly withdrawal limits or profit thresholds.

5. Risk Management Tools: Position limits, maximum leverage, and restricted symbols are enforced at the broker integration level. You can't accidentally violate rules because the platform won't let you place a trade that breaks your account guidelines. This enforcement happens automatically, reducing operational risk for traders new to prop trading.

6. Phidias Wallet Fast Payouts: Withdrawals process in 1–4 hours during business days. Traditional prop firms take 5–10 business days. For day traders, this speed matters—your capital is back in your control quickly, enabling reinvestment or personal use without extended waiting periods.

7. News Trading Permission: Unlike many prop firms that restrict news trading, Phidias permits news trading on all account types. You can trade futures around FOMC announcements, CPI releases, or earnings seasons without violating your account rules, as long as you stay within position limits.

Phidias PropFirm Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Phidias offers four fixed account tiers with one-time evaluation fees and no monthly subscriptions:

  • Static 25K ($55/month equivalent cost): $25,000 funded account with one-time $55 fee. Best for beginners testing prop trading without major capital commitment. Profit split is typically 70%, making monthly profit of $1,000 yield $700 take-home. Limited leverage (usually 1:10) and smaller position limits.
  • Fundamental 50K ($116/month equivalent): $50,000 account with $116 one-time fee. Better for intermediate traders ready to scale. Profit split reaches 75–80%. Position limits and leverage increase, letting you trade larger size in futures contracts. Breaks even after just 2 profitable months compared to subscription firms.
  • Fundamental 100K ($145/month equivalent): $100,000 account for serious day traders and scalpers. One-time $145 fee with 80–85% profit split. Full leverage and position limits appropriate for multi-contract scalping strategies. Generates significant monthly income for consistent traders.
  • Fundamental 150K ($173/month equivalent): Top tier $150,000 account with $173 one-time fee and up to 90% profit split. For professionals running multiple funded accounts or high-volume scalping. Maximum leverage and position limits. On $10K monthly profit, you keep $9,000—the highest take-home of any Phidias tier.

Pricing comparison: If you trade for 12 months, Phidias costs $55–$173 total. A subscription-based firm like Funded Trading Plus charges $99–$399/month, meaning annual costs of $1,188–$4,788. Phidias becomes cheaper after your first profitable month, making it ideal if you can consistently generate profit and don't need help learning to trade profitably.

Recommendation by trader type: Beginners should start with Static 25K ($55) to validate their strategy without big fees. Intermediate scalpers should move to Fundamental 50K ($116) to trade larger size and boost income. Experienced day traders should grab Fundamental 100K ($145) or 150K ($173) to maximize profit split on high-volume strategies.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Phidias PropFirm

  • Use EOD drawdown to your advantage: If you scalp or day trade, expect and plan for intraday dips. The EOD rule means you have the entire trading session to recover before it counts. Trade aggressively intraday and manage your exit around 30 minutes before close to ensure you're flat or profitable by end of day.
  • Stack multiple funded accounts: There's no rule against running two or three Phidias accounts simultaneously. If you have capital, open a 25K and 50K account ($171 total, one-time). Run different strategies on each account or scale your best strategy. Your total monthly cost is still $0 after the one-time fees.
  • Optimize for your profit split tier: Each tier has breakpoints for profit split (70% at $55 account, 75–80% at $116, etc.). If you're close to a tier breakpoint in monthly profit, consider upgrading your account to lock in higher profit share. A $50K account at 80% split generates more take-home than $25K at 70%, even accounting for the higher fee.
  • Monitor drawdown in real-time: Check your Phidias dashboard during trading hours to track your running drawdown. The EOD rule gives you grace, but breaching your max drawdown limit still ends your account. Set alerts at 50% and 75% drawdown so you can adjust risk if you're trending toward a breach.
  • Trade the news on Phidias accounts: Major competitors restrict news trading. Phidias permits it on all tiers. If you have a proven news trading strategy, this is a competitive advantage. Add economic calendar trading or earnings plays to your strategy and boost profits without violating account rules.
  • Leverage the Rithmic integration: Phidias only works with Rithmic-compatible platforms, which creates a small learning curve if you've been using MetaTrader or TradingView. But Rithmic offers superior order execution and position monitoring for futures. Invest 2–3 hours learning Rithmic hotkeys—the speed gains will pay back in reduced slippage.
  • Withdraw profits daily to de-risk: Phidias allows daily payouts via Phidias Wallet. Don't leave all your profits on the funded account. Withdraw daily or weekly to your personal bank account, de-risking your capital and ensuring you've genuinely profitable (not just lucky). Reinvest only what you're comfortable risking.

Common Phidias PropFirm Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: Broker integration won't connect or keeps timing out. Phidias requires Rithmic-compatible brokers like TD Ameritrade (ThinkorSwim), TradeStation, or Lightspeed. If integration fails, verify your broker supports Rithmic, that your login credentials are correct, and that you don't have two-factor authentication blocking the connection on your broker account. Disable 2FA on your broker temporarily during setup, reconnect, then re-enable it once linked.

Issue 2: You breached the evaluation profit target but didn't pass. Phidias evaluations require hitting a profit target AND staying within drawdown limits for the full evaluation period (usually 7–14 days). If you hit profit but breached drawdown once, you restart. Review the exact drawdown threshold in your account rules and trade smaller size next attempt. The EOD rule should help—focus on closing profitable or flat daily, and drawdown violations become rare.

Issue 3: Payouts are delayed beyond the 1–4 hour window. Weekend or after-hours withdrawals process on the next business day. If it's a weekday morning and your payout hasn't arrived after 4 hours, check that your bank IBAN is correct in your Phidias Wallet settings. Contact Phidias support with your withdrawal transaction ID; they typically resolve delays within 24 hours.

Issue 4: Your account got closed for a rule violation you didn't realize. Phidias enforces position limits, leverage caps, and restricted symbols automatically at the broker integration level, but the enforcement can be strict. Re-read your account's Trading Rules tab before each trading session. If you're unsure whether a trade is allowed (e.g., micro E-mini vs full-size ES contract), test placing it on demo first or email Phidias support to confirm it's within limits.

Is Phidias PropFirm Worth It? Our Verdict

Phidias PropFirm delivers genuine value if you're a consistent, profitable futures day trader or scalper who trades frequently enough to justify the one-time fee within 1–3 months. The 3.9/5 rating reflects solid performance, fast payouts, and transparent EOD drawdown rules that favor active traders. However, it's futures-only—no stock trading, spot crypto, or traditional forex—so you'll miss diversification if those are your preferred markets. The firm is also young (founded 2023), so long-term track record is limited compared to established competitors like Funded Trading Plus or Phidias vs TopStep. New traders should start with the $55 Static 25K tier to validate their edge, then scale to larger accounts once consistently profitable. Experienced scalpers running multiple strategies will thrive on Fundamental 100K or 150K accounts with 80–90% profit splits. Skip Phidias if you trade equities, want monthly subscription flexibility, or prefer established firms with decade-long histories.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
